title: A Brownfields Success Story – The Golovin Fish Plant Project
summary: The community of Golovin sits on a small peninsula in Northwest Alaska between two large bodies of water. Approximately 240 people reside in Golovin these days, but 60 years ago, during the fish processing heyday when many jobs were available, the community was more than double in size. In the early 1960’s, the Golovin Fish Co-op was developed by AYK Fisheries and they promised jobs and a bright future with decades of processing...
